title: locale.localeconv(): concurrent calls race (heap-use-after-free) via the non-thread-safe C localeconv() static struct -- = cpython#127081 (thread-unsafe libc functions)
description: locale.localeconv() (Modules/_localemodule.c: _locale_localeconv_impl -> locale_decode_monetary) calls the C library localeconv(), which returns a pointer to a SINGLE static `struct lconv`, then strdup()s / decodes its string fields (mon_decimal_point, currency_symbol, ...). C localeconv() is not thread-safe (POSIX: the returned struct may be overwritten by a subsequent call in another thread). Two threads in localeconv() concurrently race on the shared static lconv fields -- TSan reports a heap-use-after-free (one thread's _PyMem_Strdup reads a C string that another thread's strdup()/localeconv() overwrote/freed). CPython does not lock around the C localeconv() + field reads. This is an instance of cpython#127081 ('Thread-unsafe libc functions').
sites: Objects/obmalloc.c _PyMem_Strdup:1331 (READ a C string from the static lconv, via locale_decode_monetary <- _locale_localeconv_impl)
libc strdup / localeconv() (WRITE/overwrite the static `struct lconv` fields in another thread's localeconv() call)
race_kind: thread-unsafe libc call: concurrent localeconv() calls race the single static `struct lconv` the C library returns -> a use-after-free of its string fields (read vs the overwriting strdup). CPython wraps the non-thread-safe libc localeconv() without a lock.
shared_state: the C library's static `struct lconv` (returned by localeconv()) and its strdup'd string fields
severity: moderate (memory-unsafe: TSan reports heap-use-after-free), but the root cause is libc's non-thread-safe localeconv(), and the mitigation is CPython-side locking. Filed upstream as cpython#127081 (thread-unsafe libc functions).
repro_reliability: Fleet-12 surfaced it (2 veh, via locale.localeconv()). Not reproduced in isolation this session -- triggering it needs a locale whose monetary fields are non-empty (locale-dependent); the C-locale default has empty monetary strings so nothing is strdup'd. The fleet vehicles ran under a locale with monetary data.
suggested_fix: Lock around the C localeconv() call + the reads of its result in _locale_localeconv_impl (a module-level or interpreter-level lock), or snapshot the struct under the lock before decoding. This is the CPython-side half of cpython#127081 (the libc function itself cannot be made thread-safe portably).
prior_art: cpython#127081 ('Thread-unsafe libc functions', OPEN) is the umbrella for CPython wrappers of non-thread-safe libc functions (localeconv, and others). This localeconv UAF is an instance.
related: Thread-unsafe-libc class (cpython#127081), distinct from the CPython-object races. Surfaced via --tsan-no-halt (fleet-12).
notes: Known (= cpython#127081). Cataloged for dedup. Memory-unsafe (UAF) but libc-rooted; the fix is CPython-side locking. Not a new filing. NOTE: the signature `heap-use-after-free Objects/obmalloc.c:_PyMem_Strdup` is somewhat generic -- verify locale_decode_monetary / localeconv is on the stack before trusting this label.
